hooks.command: input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | grep -o '"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/'); if echo "$file" | grep -qE '(\.eslintrc|\.eslint\.config|eslint\.config|tsconfig.*\.json|\.editorconfig)$'; then echo "Blocked: fix the code, not the linter config." >&2; exit 2; fi; exit 0

input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | grep -o '"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/'); if echo "$file" | grep -qE '\.md$'; then if ! echo "$file" | grep -qE '(planning/|\.claude/|CLAUDE\.md$|MODIFICATIONS\.md$|PROJECT_PLAN\.md$)'; then echo "Blocked: .md writes should go in planning/, .claude/, or be a root doc. Path: $file" >&2; exit 2; fi; fi; exit 0

input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | grep -o '"command"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"command"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -q '\-\-no-verify'; then echo "Blocked: --no-verify bypasses git hooks. Fix the underlying issue instead." >&2; exit 2; fi; exit 0

input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | grep -o '"command"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"command"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'git\s+push'; then if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'git\s+push.*\b(main|master)\b'; then echo "WARNING: Pushing directly to main/master. Confirm this is intentional." >&2; fi; fi; exit 0

input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | grep -o '"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/'); if [ -n "$file" ] && echo "$file" | grep -qE '\.(ts|js|tsx|jsx)$'; then if grep -n 'console\.log' "$file" 2>/dev/null | head -5; then echo "WARNING: console.log statements detected. Remove before committing." >&2; fi; fi; exit 0

echo "--- Pre-compaction snapshot ---"; echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)"; echo "Commit: $(git log --oneline -1 2>/dev/null)"; echo "Modified:"; git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | head -15 || echo '  none'; exit 0

hooks.prompt: Context compaction is about to happen. Before it does, use the secretary agent to: (1) Write any important research findings or facts from this session to the relevant agent's memory file (.claude/agent-memory/<agent>.md), (2) Update planning/SESSION_HANDOFF.md with current task state and next steps only — keep it lean, no knowledge dumps. SESSION_HANDOFF.md is @-imported in CLAUDE.md so it survives compaction. Agent memory files persist on disk. Together they ensure nothing important is lost.

hooks.command: echo 'Session resumed after compaction. SESSION_HANDOFF.md is already loaded via @ import. Check agent memory files in .claude/agent-memory/ for domain-specific context if needed.'; exit 0

changed=$(git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(ts|js|tsx|jsx)$' | head -1); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo "Reminder: Source files modified since last commit. Consider running /compile before ending." >&2; fi; echo 'Session ending. Consider running /handoff to capture state.' >&2; exit 0

hooks.prompt: Evaluate whether this session produced any reusable RULES (not facts). A rule is 'when X happens, do Y' — a pattern that should become a permanent instruction. If yes, suggest adding it to CLAUDE.md or the relevant agent prompt. Facts and research findings should already be in agent memory — do not repeat them here. If no new rules, say so briefly.

hooks.command: if command -v powershell.exe >/dev/null 2>&1; then powershell.exe -Command "Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms; [System.Windows.Forms.MessageBox]::Show('Claude Code needs your attention', 'Notification', 'OK', 'Information')" 2>/dev/null; elif command -v notify-send >/dev/null 2>&1; then notify-send 'Claude Code' 'Needs your attention' 2>/dev/null; elif command -v osascript >/dev/null 2>&1; then osascript -e 'display notification "Claude Code needs your attention" with title "Notification"' 2>/dev/null; fi; exit 0
